fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Metastatic squamous non-small cell lung carcinoma (neoplasia maligna de pulmão — CID C34), with metastatic disease; the NATJUS opinion references squamous metastatic non-small cell lung carcinoma as the basis for Pembrolizumabe treatment
fields.ICD_CODE: CID C34 — malignant neoplasm of bronchus and lung (explicitly stated in the document)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient (Luiz Carlos Sardo) was diagnosed with metastatic lung adenocarcinoma (CID C34). Since December 2022, the patient has been undergoing treatment with Pembrolizumabe under a prior judicial order, presenting stable disease and good tolerance. The treating physician recommended maintenance of immunotherapy with Pembrolizumabe for an indefinite period, until disease progression and/or limiting toxicity. The original judicial decision granting the medication was subsequently reformed by the Tribunal Regional Federal da 4ª Região (TRF4), which denied provision on the ground that the CONITEC Report No. 859/2023 recommended against incorporation. The patient then filed a Reclamação before the Supremo Tribunal Federal (STF), which ultimately upheld the patient's right to the drug.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Disease progression under available SUS therapies; imminent risk of death upon interruption of Pembrolizumabe treatment
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Metastatic disease (stage not specified numerically in document); disease described as progressive and grave with imminent risk of death upon treatment interruption; alternatives offered by SUS described as inferior to ongoing therapy
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: All treatments available through the Unified Health System (SUS) trialed previously without satisfactory results; platinum-based chemotherapy recommended by CONITEC as alternative, described as inferior to Pembrolizumabe for this patient
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: The treating physician attested that first-line SUS treatment was not effective and that clinical signs of disease progression were present; discontinuation of Pembrolizumabe would carry greater risks of disease progression and death; SUS alternatives described as inferior to the ongoing therapy
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): high expression (greater than 50%) referenced in the NATJUS opinion as basis for first-line Pembrolizumabe plus chemotherapy indication; Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): not found; Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): not found; ROS Proto-Oncogene 1 (ROS1): not found; KRAS: not found; B-Raf Proto-Oncogene (BRAF): not found; Microsatellite Instability status: not found; Tumor Mutational Burden: not found; Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2): not found
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: NOT FOUND — the document identifies the disease as metastatic but does not list specific metastatic sites
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Medical reports (laudos médicos) attested to the clinical indispensability of Pembrolizumabe in urgent character, given disease progression and imminent risk of death. The treating physician indicated maintenance of immunotherapy with Pembrolizumabe indefinitely until disease progression or limiting toxicity. The NATJUS opinion confirmed that Pembrolizumabe plus chemotherapy showed substantial improvement in overall survival and progression-free survival in patients with metastatic squamous non-small cell lung carcinoma not previously treated, supporting Pembrolizumabe associated with chemotherapy as standard first-line treatment.
fields.URGENCY: Described as urgent (caráter de urgência) throughout the decision; the STF expressly found that it was not reasonable to require exhaustion of ordinary instances given imminent risk of death; the NATJUS opinion noted urgency given the potential risk to the patient's life
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: The treating physician stated that interruption of treatment could result in greater risks of disease progression; the SUS alternatives were described as inferior to the ongoing therapy
fields.RISK_DEATH: Medical documents indicate imminent risk of death (risco iminente de morte) upon discontinuation of Pembrolizumabe; this finding was determinative in both the original reclamação decision and the denial of the internal appeal
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumabe 200 mg intravenous, administered every 3 weeks, for an indefinite period until disease progression or limiting toxicity; first-line palliative immunotherapy associated with chemotherapy (carboplatin and paclitaxel)
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Primeira Turma of the Supremo Tribunal Federal, by majority, denied the internal appeal (agravo interno) filed by the State of Santa Catarina, thereby maintaining the original decision that upheld the Reclamação and ordered the provision of Pembrolizumabe
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The STF held that the exceptional conditions required by Theme 6 of general repercussion (RE 566.471) were satisfied, authorizing judicial intervention to compel provision of a non-standardized medication. The Court found that the challenged TRF4 decision contradicted item 3(b) of Theme 6 by failing to properly consult and apply the NATJUS opinion, which had confirmed the scientific basis for the medication. Additionally, the Court noted the particular circumstances of the case — the patient had been taking Pembrolizumabe since December 2022 under a prior judicial order, presenting stable disease and good tolerance — and that interruption would cause imminent risk of death, justifying waiver of the exhaustion-of-ordinary-instances requirement under articles 7, 8 and 44 of the Brazilian Constitution and the principles of the right to life and health.

rows.N: Advanced lung cancer with subcutaneous nodule metastasis

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Metastatic lung adenocarcinoma (adenocarcinoma de pulmão metastático), advanced stage with metastasis in subcutaneous nodules; CID C34
fields.ICD_CODE: CID C34 — malignant neoplasm of bronchus and lung (explicitly stated in the document)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient (Romilda Fatima da Silva) was diagnosed with advanced lung cancer with metastasis in subcutaneous nodules (CID C34). She underwent all treatments available through the SUS without satisfactory results. Platinum-based chemotherapy was recommended by CONITEC as an alternative to Pembrolizumabe, but was shown to be ineffective for this patient through medical reports and imaging exams. A request for Pembrolizumabe 200mg IV was filed administratively and denied. The patient filed an action in the lower courts, where the urgency injunction was denied despite a favorable NATJUS technical note. An interlocutory appeal (agravo de instrumento) was filed, and the suspensive effect was denied. The TRF4 denied the internal appeal on the ground that CONITEC's recommendation was unfavorable to incorporation of Pembrolizumabe for non-small cell lung cancer. The patient then filed a Reclamação before the STF on December 18, 2024, when Min. Luiz Fux granted the injunction ad referendum of the Primeira Turma, which was unanimously ratified on February 24, 2025.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Disease progression under available SUS treatments; urgent need for immunotherapy
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Advanced metastatic lung adenocarcinoma with subcutaneous nodule metastasis; imminent risk of death (risco iminente de morte); disease progression documented; described as a grave disease
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: All SUS-available treatments trialed without satisfactory results; platinum-based chemotherapy recommended by CONITEC as substitute — found ineffective for this patient, demonstrated through medical reports and imaging exams
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: The patient demonstrated through medical reports and imaging exams the inefficacy of platinum-based chemotherapy recommended by CONITEC as a substitute for Pembrolizumabe; the medication was described as the last attempt to contain the disease; clinical medical reports attested to clinical indispensability in urgent character, given disease progression
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): high expression greater than 50% referenced in NATJUS opinion as indication for first-line Pembrolizumabe; the NATJUS opinion states that for patients without driver mutations and with high PD-L1 expression (greater than 50%), first-line Pembrolizumabe is an option; Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): not found; Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): not found; ROS Proto-Oncogene 1 (ROS1): not found; KRAS: not found; B-Raf Proto-Oncogene (BRAF): not found; Microsatellite Instability status: not found; Tumor Mutational Burden: not found; Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2): not found
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Medical reports (laudos médicos) attested to the clinical indispensability of Pembrolizumabe in urgent character given disease progression and imminent risk of death. The treating physicians stated that Pembrolizumabe 200mg IV is the only current therapeutic option, with confirmed efficacy through exams, and the only treatment capable of reversing the patient's clinical condition; this immunotherapy is considered urgent and immediate-onset treatment, and non-use could allow disease advancement. The NATJUS opinion confirmed that immunotherapies when well-indicated prolong survival by up to 30 months, and that there are technical data justifying the use of Pembrolizumabe in this context, noting scientific evidence and the urgency given the potential risk to the patient's life.
fields.URGENCY: Explicitly characterized as urgent (caráter de urgência) by the treating physicians; the NATJUS opinion noted urgency given the potential risk to life; the STF found that requiring exhaustion of ordinary instances was unreasonable given the imminent risk of death
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Medical reports indicate disease progression under SUS treatments; non-use of Pembrolizumabe could allow disease advancement
fields.RISK_DEATH: Medical documents and NATJUS opinion indicate imminent risk of death (risco iminente de morte); the treating physicians stated that non-use of Pembrolizumabe could allow disease advancement, and characterized the situation as urgent with immediate onset required
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumabe 200mg intravenous, administered every 3 weeks (a cada 3 semanas), as last-line therapy; described in the medical prescription as 'the only treatment capable of reversing the clinical condition'
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Primeira Turma of the STF unanimously ratified the injunction (medida liminar) granting Pembrolizumabe, thereby suspending the TRF4 decision that had denied the medication; the Court granted provisional urgency protection
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The STF held that the exceptional conditions required by Theme 6 of general repercussion (RE 566.471) and Súmula Vinculante 61 were satisfied, authorizing judicial intervention to compel provision of Pembrolizumabe not incorporated into SUS. The Court found that the challenged TRF4 decision violated Theme 6 by refusing to grant the medication solely on the ground of the CONITEC unfavorable opinion, without analyzing whether the non-incorporation was illegal or whether there was a failure to act by CONITEC, as required by the theme's parameters. The Court noted the patient had demonstrated through medical reports and imaging the inefficacy of platinum-based chemotherapy recommended by CONITEC, the NATJUS favorable technical note, and the imminent risk of death, which together justified waiving the exhaustion-of-ordinary-instances requirement. The decision was grounded in the constitutional right to health (article 196) and life (article 5) and the parameters of Themes 6 and 1.234.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Lymphoma (patología denominada 'Linfomas en personas de 15 años o más', designated as Problem No. 17 under Decree No. 4 of 2013); specific subtype not further detailed in document; patient referred to as 'Noah'

fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (referred to as 'Noah'), spouse of the appellant, was hospitalized at Clínica Las Condes where Pembrolizumab was administered as treatment for the lymphoma. The patient subsequently died on April 15, 2016. The treatment was prescribed by treating physicians as an alternative therapeutic option for the disease. The Isapre Cruz Blanca denied coverage of Pembrolizumab on the ground that it was not listed in the 2016 Free Choice Schedule (Arancel 2016 en modalidad Libre Elección) approved by Resolution Exenta No. 16 of January 7, 2016, and lacked health registry at the Instituto de Salud Pública at the time of administration.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: NOT FOUND — described as a catastrophic disease condition warranting special coverage
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physicians determined that Pembrolizumab constituted an alternative treatment for the lymphoma that afflicted the patient (Noah). The Guía Clínica Auge Linfoma personas de 15 años o más itself acknowledged that treatments are not exhaustive and that advances in scientific knowledge may lead to changes in treatment, delegating determination of treatment to qualified professionals — a situation that occurred in this case.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: NOT FOUND — patient deceased at time of judicial decision
fields.RISK_DEATH: NOT FOUND — patient deceased on April 15, 2016, during the relevant period
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab — dose and route not specified in document; administered during hospitalization at Clínica Las Condes; associated with medical program folio No. 5140570
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tercera Sala of the Corte Suprema of Chile revoked the first-instance ruling and ordered Isapre Cruz Blanca to provide GES-CAEC coverage for the treatment received by Noah with Pembrolizumab associated with medical program folio No. 5140570
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Corte Suprema held that the Isapre's refusal to cover Pembrolizumab lacked reasonableness and violated the constitutional guarantees under article 19 No. 2 and No. 24 of the Constitución Política. The Court reasoned that although Pembrolizumab was not in the GES basket and lacked registration at the Instituto de Salud Pública at the time of administration, Circular IF No. 7 of July 1, 2005 of the Superintendencia de Salud provides for special GES-CAEC coverage for treatments recognized in clinical protocols defined by the Ministerio de Salud even if not listed in the GES schedule. The Court further noted that the Guía Clínica Auge Linfoma contemplates changes based on scientific advances, Pembrolizumab was approved by the FDA in 2016 for treatment of patients with recurrent or metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of head and neck, and the drug had a valid health registry in Chile since June 24, 2016, demonstrating it was within the scientific advances contemplated by the clinical guide.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Artículo 19 No. 2 y No. 24 de la Constitución Política de la República; artículo 20 de la Constitución Política de la República; artículo 5° del Decreto Supremo de Salud No. 4 de 2013 (Garantías Explícitas en Salud); artículo 59 del DFL No. 1 (registro sanitario); Circular IF No. 7 de 1° de julio de 2005 de la Superintendencia de Salud (artículo 2° de su anexo sobre Condiciones de la Cobertura Adicional para Enfermedades Catastróficas — GES-CAEC); Resolución Exenta No. 16 del 7 de enero de 2016 (Arancel 2016 Modalidad Libre Elección); Guía Clínica Auge Linfoma personas de 15 años o más; FDA approval 2016 for Pembrolizumab in recurrent or metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of head and neck; Auto Acordado de la Corte Suprema sobre Recurso de Protección

rows.N: LUNG CANCER with clinical staging close to T4 due to malignant tumor in the main bronchus

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Malignant tumor of the main bronchus (tumor maligno del bronquio principal), with clinical staging described as close to T4; lung cancer, non-small cell type based on the triple marker panel result context
fields.SECONDARY_DIAGNOSES: Borderline performance status (performance limítrofe); marked state of fragility (marcado estado de fragilidad) — documented by the treating oncologist
fields.ICD_CODE: NOT FOUND — the document refers to 'tumor maligno de bronquio principal' and 'tumor maligno del bronquio principal' but does not state an ICD code
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Mr. Aníbal Trujillo Parra, 65 years old, was diagnosed with malignant tumor of the main bronchus. From February 2020, he was treated by oncology specialists at ESE Hospital Hernando Moncaleano Perdomo de Neiva and thoracic specialists at IPS MEDILASER S.A. On February 26, 2020, thoracic surgery was performed for resection of mediastinal tumor by thoracoscopy, but due to the large size of the tumor, only tissue samples could be extracted for pathology. On March 11, 2020, the oncology specialist ordered standard chemotherapy protocol every 21 days; treatment began March 16, with second session April 6, and last session April 27, 2020. At the May 15, 2020 control consultation, chemotherapy protocol was suspended due to no objective response to treatment, with a slight volumetric increase of the mass observed. On March 27, 2020, a triple lung marker panel (EGFR+ALK+PDL-1) performed by Fundación para la Investigación Clínica y Molecular Aplicada del Cáncer (FIMAC) yielded results confirming the patient as a candidate for second-line immunotherapy treatment. On May 20, 2020, the oncology specialist at ESE Hospital Universitario Hernando Moncaleano Perdomo de Neiva ordered urgent initiation of immunotherapy with Pembrolizumab 200mg IV every 21 days for 6 cycles (6 months, 24 ampoules). Nueva EPS authorized the treatment 'Politerapia Antineoplásica de Alta Toxicidad' on May 20, 2020, under authorization No. 0833-127146890, but failed to deliver the medication. The patient's family purchased two Pembrolizumab ampoules for the first cycle (May 29, 2020) at a cost of $21,046,854, and again two ampoules for the second cycle (June 23, 2020) at a cost of $21,046,854, due to non-delivery by the EPS. A tutela action was filed on behalf of the patient.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Difficulty breathing; difficulty speaking; difficulty eating under normal conditions
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Clinical staging close to T4 due to malignant tumor in the main bronchus; borderline performance status; marked state of fragility; not a candidate for chemo-radiotherapy protocols nor for combination of cytotoxic therapy with immunotherapy; second-line treatment with palliative intent indicated
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: Difficulty breathing, speaking, and eating under normal conditions; unable to work since February 2020
fields.COMORBIDITIES: Borderline performance status; marked state of fragility (fragilidad)
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Standard chemotherapy protocol every 21 days (3 cycles: March 16, April 6, and April 27, 2020) — outcome: no objective response to treatment, with slight volumetric increase of the mass; treatment suspended on May 15, 2020
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: After three cycles of chemotherapy, no objective response was observed and a slight volumetric increase of the mass was documented; the treating oncologist concluded that the patient was not a candidate for chemo-radiotherapy protocols nor for combination of cytotoxic therapy with immunotherapy; Pembrolizumab was indicated as the only second-line treatment with palliative intent, with no equivalent medication in the Plan de Beneficios de Salud (PBS)
fields.BIOMARKERS: Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): negative (Mutaciones en el GEN EGFR: Negativo); Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): negative (Inmunoreactividad para ALK: Negativo); Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): positive at 95% (Inmunoreactividad para PD-L1: Positivo TPS 95%); Microsatellite Instability status: not found; Tumor Mutational Burden: not found; Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2): not found; ROS Proto-Oncogene 1 (ROS1): not found; KRAS: not found; B-Raf Proto-Oncogene (BRAF): not found
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist specialist concluded that the patient, given his borderline performance status, marked state of fragility, and clinical staging close to T4, was not a candidate for chemo-radiotherapy protocols nor for combination of cytotoxic therapy with immunotherapy. Second-line treatment with palliative intent was indicated, specifically the immunotherapy protocol with Pembrolizumab 200mg IV every 21 days for 6 months. The triple marker panel (EGFR+ALK+PDL-1) confirmed that the patient was a candidate for second-line immunotherapy with a Programmed Death-Ligand 1 positivity rate of 95%, representing a high probability of treatment efficacy. No equivalent medication existed in the Plan de Beneficios de Salud.
fields.URGENCY: The medical prescription expressly marked the treatment as 'URGENT' (URGENTE); the treating oncologist ordered the treatment in an urgent manner; the court found the patient was a subject of special constitutional protection given his age (adult over 65) and catastrophic illness diagnosis (cancer)
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Interruption in treatment continuity risked compromising the patient's life; the oncologist had ordered treatment marked as urgent; disease was progressing under chemotherapy
fields.RISK_DEATH: The document references that the patient's right to life was threatened by the EPS's failure to provide the medication; the court noted that cancer is a catastrophic illness requiring special constitutional protection; the patient had to purchase medications at personal cost totaling $42,093,708 to receive the first two cycles
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200mg intravenous, Day 1, every 21 days; total treatment: 6 months, 6 cycles, 24 ampoules; treatment protocol designated as 'Politerapia Antineoplásica de Alta Toxicidad'; second-line immunotherapy with palliative intent
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — the Tribunal Contencioso Administrativo del Huila, Sala Segunda de Decisión confirmed the first-instance ruling: (1) upheld the tutela protection of fundamental rights to human dignity, life, and health of Aníbal Trujillo Parra; (2) confirmed the order to Nueva EPS to provide integral treatment including Pembrolizumab 200mg; (3) declared the tutela action inadmissible regarding the claim for reimbursement of medication costs for the two cycles purchased by the family
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal held that Nueva EPS violated the patient's fundamental rights to health, life, and human dignity through unjustified delays in authorizing, supplying, and delivering the Pembrolizumab medication prescribed urgently by the treating oncology specialist, causing the patient's family to purchase medications at their own expense. The Court applied the principle of integrality established in the Estatutaria de Salud (Law 1751 of 2015, article 8), under which the State and healthcare entities are obligated to guarantee complete authorization of all treatments, medications, interventions, procedures, examinations, controls, follow-ups, and other services the patient requires for care of his pathology. The Court further held that cancer is a catastrophic illness requiring special integral attention regardless of whether the required services are excluded from the mandatory plans, and confirmed that the order for integral treatment was proper to prevent successive tutela actions for each individually prescribed service.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Catastrophic illness (enfermedad catastrófica) — specific diagnosis not identified in this procedural decision; the underlying case involved treatment with Pembrolizumab which was acquired by the patient for emergent treatment
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Rosibel Alexandra Peña Narváez, represented by Yajaira Curipallo Álava (Delegada Provincial de la Defensoría del Pueblo Pastaza), filed a protective action with precautionary measures on April 13, 2021, against the Director General of the Instituto Ecuatoriano de Seguridad Social (IESS) and the legal representative of SOLCA Núcleo Tungurahua, alleging violation of rights to health, to persons and priority groups, to availability and accessibility of medications, to quality and safe medications, and to a dignified life, due to failure to provide priority and timely treatment for her catastrophic illness. The patient acquired Pembrolizumab for emergent self-treatment. The first-instance court on June 7, 2021 accepted the action and declared rights violations, ordering delivery of the medication for continued treatment. The patient appealed for clarification and expansion; the IESS appealed. On June 21, 2021, clarification and expansion requests were denied; the patient appealed. On July 14, 2021, the Sala Multicompetente of the Corte Provincial de Justicia de Pastaza denied the IESS's appeal and accepted the patient's appeal, reforming the judgment regarding economic reparation measures. On August 12, 2021, the IESS filed an extraordinary protection action before the Corte Constitucional against the July 14, 2021 ruling.

fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Corte Constitucional's Admission Panel inadmitted the extraordinary protection action because the IESS failed to develop a clear legal argument. The Court found that: (1) the IESS merely alleged that the appellate court did not consider certain administrative regulations (Acuerdo No. 0091-2017, Resolución IESS 308, Acuerdo MSP No. 0158-A-2017) without justifying how the judges' actions directly and immediately violated constitutional rights; and (2) the IESS's claim that the court failed to apply applicable law was inadmissible under article 62, numeral 4 of the Ley Orgánica de Garantías Jurisdiccionales y Control Constitucional, which prohibits founding an extraordinary protection action on incorrect application or non-application of law. The Constitutional Court emphasized that it lacks competence to analyze the correct or incorrect application of legal norms by lower judges.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Triple-negative breast cancer (cáncer de mama triple negativo); neoadjuvant disease stage — not described as advanced or metastatic at diagnosis
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (anonymized as 'AA'), 34 years old, is treated at ASSE Maldonado by Dr. Florencia Savio. Based on studies performed, neoadjuvant treatment was indicated with chemotherapy comprising carboplatin plus paclitaxel plus Pembrolizumab. The proposed regimen was neoadjuvant polychemotherapy based on paclitaxel and carboplatin with Pembrolizumab for four cycles followed by adjuvant Pembrolizumab, based on scientific evidence from the Phase III study denominated Keynote 522. The chemotherapy component (carboplatin and paclitaxel) is included in the Plan Integral de Atención en Salud (PIAS) and covered by her health provider. Pembrolizumab is a high-cost medication and the patient demonstrated economic impossibility of payment. Both the treating physician and a medical committee (ateneo médico) agreed that the patient must receive this drug. The patient had no other treatment included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos (FTM), the PIAS, or the Fondo Nacional de Recursos (FNR) that could replace Pembrolizumab. The first-instance court (Sentencia No. 1 of January 5, 2026) ordered the FNR to supply Pembrolizumab and dismissed the action against the Ministerio de Salud Pública (MSP). The Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Segundo Turno, by Sentencia No. 10/2026 of February 9, 2026, revoked the first-instance ruling, dismissing FNR's passive legitimacy and condemning the MSP to supply Pembrolizumab.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Triple-negative breast cancer requiring neoadjuvant treatment; the court acknowledged the gravity of the patient's health state and the urgency of the therapeutic indication; not described as advanced or metastatic — neoadjuvant stage
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: None stated — this is a neoadjuvant treatment plan (no prior systemic therapy for the breast cancer)
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND — this is a newly diagnosed case with no prior treatment; it was established that no other treatment included in the FTM, PIAS, or FNR covered the patient's therapeutic need
fields.BIOMARKERS: Triple-negative breast cancer biomarker profile implies Estrogen Receptor negative, Progesterone Receptor negative, Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) negative (by definition of triple-negative); Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): not stated; Microsatellite Instability status: not stated; Tumor Mutational Burden: not stated
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: NOT FOUND — disease described as not advanced or metastatic
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Both the treating physician (Dr. Florencia Savio) and the medical committee (ateneo médico) agreed that the patient must receive Pembrolizumab. The indication is based on scientific evidence from the Phase III Keynote 522 study. No other treatment covered by the FTM, PIAS, or FNR was available as an alternative. None of the respondents contested the patient's medical situation, diagnosis, gravity of health state, or the appropriateness of the therapeutic indication.
fields.URGENCY: The gravity of the patient's health condition determined — as is notorious — that there is no time available and an immediate and adequate response is required; the court noted that delay in receiving this pharmacological treatment carries an actual and effective threat to the patient's right to health
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Delay in receiving treatment would constitute an actual and effective threat to the patient's right to health with a risk of irreparable or difficult-to-repair harm
fields.RISK_DEATH: The non-supply of the medication or delay in receiving this pharmacological treatment constitutes an actual and effective threat to the patient's right to health and life; the Tribunal grounded its decision on the patient's right to life under articles 7, 8, and 44 of the Uruguayan Constitution
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab — neoadjuvant combination with paclitaxel and carboplatin for four cycles, followed by adjuvant Pembrolizumab; dose, route, and frequency according to treating physician's indications; based on Phase III Keynote 522 study
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Segundo Turno (Sentencia No. 10/2026), by unanimous decision with majority grounds, revoked the first-instance ruling and: (1) upheld the FNR's exception of lack of passive legitimacy (dismissing the action against FNR); and (2) condemned the MSP to supply Pembrolizumab to the patient in accordance with the treating physician's indications and for the duration the physician requires, within 24 hours
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal found that the MSP's refusal to provide the medication constituted manifestly illegitimate conduct because it was based on administrative and/or economic reasons without attending to scientific criteria or the individual patient's situation. The Court held that the MSP's high responsibilities for preserving health and life cannot be conditioned by economic or sustainability reasons when the medication's efficacy for the patient's illness was not disputed. Denying coverage would violate equality since patients with financial means can access the medication privately while equally ill patients who cannot afford it cannot. The FNR was discharged because its cometido is restricted to financing medications and therapies under its legally regulated mandate, and it cannot be compelled to act beyond its legal competence (Ley No. 16.343, Decree No. 358/993, Art. 313 of Ley No. 17.930, Decree No. 265/006). The decision was grounded in articles 7, 44, 72 and 332 of the Constitution, Law 18.335, Law 18.211, article 12 of the Pacto Internacional de los Derechos Económicos, Sociales y Culturales, and the Protocolo de San Salvador.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Artículos 7, 8, 44, 72 y 332 de la Constitución de la República Oriental del Uruguay; Ley No. 16.011 (Acción de Amparo); Ley No. 16.343 (Fondo Nacional de Recursos); Decreto reglamentario No. 358/993; Art. 313 de la Ley No. 17.930; Decreto No. 265/006; artículos 409 y 410 de la LUC (Ley de Urgente Consideración); artículo 12 del Pacto Internacional de los Derechos Económicos, Sociales y Culturales; Protocolo de San Salvador; Ley 18.335; Ley 18.211; artículo 10 del Código Civil (derogación tácita); TAC 2o Turno Sentencia No. 209/009; TAC 2o Turno Sentencia 32/2021; TAC 2o Turno Sentencia del 24/VIII/2020; TAC 2o Turno Sentencia No. 221/2020; Sentencia SEF-0005-000144/2016; Sentencia SEF 0005-000003/2015 TAC 2; LARENZ, Der. fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal held that the FNR's refusal to provide medications that are included in the FTM for oncological diseases and for which it had agreed to provide financing cannot be founded on formal administrative distinctions lacking scientific and medical justification. The Court cited TAC 2o precedent: when a medication is covered by the FNR for oncological diseases, the FNR cannot refuse on formal grounds — doing so would violate the principle of equality by establishing an unjust and senseless discrimination depriving the patient of previously agreed rights. The FNR's position that the drugs were only covered for certain specific conditions (not the patient's specific condition) was rejected because the distinction lacked medical-scientific basis. Constitutional rights to health, life, and equality under articles 7, 8, 10, and 44 of the Uruguayan Constitution were invoked.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: High-grade urothelial carcinoma (carcinoma urotelial de alto grado) of the bladder; confirmed by biopsy of bladder tumor with pathological anatomy
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (anonymized as 'AA'), 73 years old, is treated at SMI (Sociedad Médica del Interior). She consulted on multiple occasions for polyuria. In February 2025, a tomography, magnetic resonance imaging, bone scintigraphy, and biopsy of bladder tumor were performed; pathological anatomy confirmed the diagnosis of high-grade urothelial carcinoma. In this context, treatment based on Pembrolizumab and Enfortumab Vedotin was indicated. Pembrolizumab is in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos (FTM) for other pathologies but not for the patient's condition. Enfortumab Vedotin is not registered in Uruguay for any pathology. Both medications were requested from the respondents without favorable results. The patient lacks sufficient economic resources to cover the high cost, but offered to pay $40,000 monthly. The first-instance court (Sentencia No. 29/2025) ordered the MSP to supply Enfortumab Vedotin and ordered the FNR to supply Pembrolizumab. The Tribunal (Sentencia No. 151/2025, July 28, 2025) partially reversed: (1) confirmed condemnation of MSP for Enfortumab Vedotin; (2) revoked the FNR condemnation and instead condemned the MSP also to supply Pembrolizumab; (3) the Suprema Corte de Justicia had previously declared unconstitutional the second paragraph of article 7 of Law 18.335 and the final paragraph of article 45 of Law 18.211 as applied to this patient.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Polyuria (recurring episodes leading to initial consultations)
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the bladder; current disease stage not described beyond the pathological anatomy confirming high-grade carcinoma; treatment indication reflects advanced or high-risk stage requiring combination immunotherapy
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: NOT FOUND — the document does not describe any prior systemic treatments for the urothelial carcinoma
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND explicitly; however, the indication for both Pembrolizumab and Enfortumab Vedotin suggests prior treatment context as the treatment is for advanced urothelial carcinoma
fields.BIOMARKERS: NOT FOUND — no specific biomarker results stated in this decision
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating physician Dr. Krygier and a court-appointed expert (Dr. Amarillo) confirmed through testimony and expert report that the treatment indication was correct, that the medications were the best scientific option for the patient's case, and that both medications showed positive effects in this patient (as confirmed by a medical report submitted during the appellate proceeding showing the patient was receiving treatment with good tolerance). None of the respondents objected to the clinical situation, diagnosis, health severity, or the appropriateness of the indicated treatment.
fields.URGENCY: Not explicitly stated but characterized as urgent through the 24-hour delivery requirement; the patient's current disease state did not allow waiting for administrative proceedings that could take months or years
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: NOT FOUND explicitly stated as risk
fields.RISK_DEATH: The right to life is invoked as a constitutional right under article 44 of the Uruguayan Constitution; denial of effective treatment constitutes an attack on the right to life; the patient's disease (high-grade urothelial carcinoma) is implicitly characterized as life-threatening
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab — dose and route per treating physician's indication, for duration required; Enfortumab Vedotin — dose and route per treating physician's indication; patient offered co-payment of $40,000 monthly; both medications to be supplied within 24 hours of presentation of medical prescription
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Quinto Turno (Sentencia No. 151/2025, July 28, 2025) unanimously partially reversed the first-instance ruling: (1) revoked the FNR condemnation to provide Pembrolizumab; (2) condemned the MSP to provide Pembrolizumab in the conditions established in the first-instance ruling; (3) confirmed the MSP condemnation to provide Enfortumab Vedotin; all without special procedural costs
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal held that the MSP's refusal to provide both medications constituted manifestly illegitimate conduct because the MSP did not invoke any scientific basis to contradict the proven evidence of the benefits of the medication in the patient's concrete case, failing to discharge its burden of proving facts modifying or extinguishing the patient's claim. The Suprema Corte de Justicia had declared unconstitutional articles relied upon by the MSP (article 7, paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and the final paragraph of article 45 of Law 18.211), rendering the MSP's formal defenses inapplicable. The constitutional mandate of article 44 (second sentence) requiring the State to provide free prevention and assistance means to the indigent or those lacking sufficient resources directly obligated the MSP. The FNR was discharged because Pembrolizumab had not been included in the FTM for this patient's specific pathological condition (only for other pathologies per Ordenanza 848/2024), and the FNR as a non-governmental public entity can only act within its legally defined mandate (Laws 16.343, 17.930, 18.211; Decrees 358/993 and 265/006).
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Artículos 7, 72 y 332 de la Constitución de la República; artículo 44 de la Constitución; Ley 16.011 (Acción de Amparo); Ley No. 16.343; Decreto reglamentario No. 358/993; Art. 313 de la Ley No. 17.930; Decreto No. 265/006; Decreto Ley No. 15.443 (obligatoriedad del registro de medicamentos); artículo 461 de la Ley No. 19.355; artículo 45 de la Ley No. 18.211; artículo 7 de la Ley No. 18.335; Ley No. 18.211 (Sistema Nacional Integrado de Salud); SCJ Sentencia No. 594/2025 del 03/06/2025 (declaró inconstitucionales artículo 7 inciso 2 de Ley 18.335 y artículo 45 inciso final de Ley 18.211); Ordenanza 848/2024 (MSP); arts. 137, 139.1, 140, 141, 154, 184, 197 y 198 del Código General del Proceso; arts. 56 y 688 del Código Civil; sentencia DFA-0004-000878/2013; T.A.C. 1o, Sentencia 203/2014; T.A.C. 6o Sentencia No. 209/2009; artículos 157 y 158 del Código General del Proceso

rows.O: Stage 4 lung cancer. Pembrolizumab in combination with platinum-based chemotherapy and pemetrexed.

fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (anonymized as 'AA'), 68 years old, is diagnosed with lung adenocarcinoma Stage Four. Treatment based on Pemetrexed and Pembrolizumab was indicated. The cost of medication was approximately $189,596 plus taxes and $14,000 plus taxes as of February 10, 2025. The patient's income does not permit private acquisition. An expert report from the Instituto Técnico Forense (fs. 103-109) confirmed the medical indication is correct and consistent with available scientific evidence and international oncology guidelines. The MSP did not contest the appropriateness of the requested treatment. The Ministerio de Salud Pública (MSP) had included Pembrolizumab in the FTM by Ordenanza No. 173/2020 for non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma, with financial coverage by the FNR, but only for patients with Programmed Death-Ligand 1 expression equal to or greater than 50% and without Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor mutations or ALK translocation. The first-instance court (Sentencia No. 16/2025 of March 6, 2025) condemned the MSP to supply Pemetrexed and Pembrolizumab and sustained the FNR's exception of lack of passive legitimacy. The Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Séptimo Turno (Sentencia No. 126/2025, June 27, 2025) confirmed the first-instance ruling. During the appellate proceedings, the patient reported receiving treatment with excellent response.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Lung adenocarcinoma Stage Four; medical indication is consistent with international oncology guidelines for advanced/metastatic disease
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND — the document does not describe prior treatment failure
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): not stated for this patient, but the FNR's internal protocol restricts coverage to patients with PD-L1 equal to or greater than 50%; the MSP's Ordenanza 173/2020 and the FNR's protocol specify the drug for cases with PD-L1 equal to or greater than 50% (TPS) and without EGFR mutation or ALK translocation; the document indicates the patient's PD-L1 status does not reach the 50% threshold required by the FNR protocol (otherwise FNR would have been condemned rather than discharged); Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): negative (implied by protocol context — MSP report indicates PD-L1 equal to or greater than 50% patients must also lack EGFR mutations and ALK translocation); Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): not explicitly stated; Microsatellite Instability status: not found; Tumor Mutational Burden: not found; Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2): not found; KRAS: not found; B-Raf Proto-Oncogene (BRAF): not found; ROS Proto-Oncogene 1 (ROS1): not found
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The expert report from the Instituto Técnico Forense confirmed the medical indication is correct and consistent with available scientific evidence and international oncology guidelines. The MSP did not contest the appropriateness of the treatment. The treating physician prescribed Pemetrexed and Pembrolizumab. All three Tribunal members (Dr. Ettlin, Dra. Cabrera, Dra. Pera) concurred in affirming the MSP condemnation, each on somewhat distinct legal bases: Dr. Ettlin grounded on constitutional values and human rights treaties; Dra. Cabrera on the equality principle under article 8 of the Constitution regarding registered medications; Dra. Pera on the unconstitutionality of limiting statutory provisions as declared by the SCJ.
fields.URGENCY: The patient's current health state does not permit waiting for administrative proceedings that could take months or years; characterized by urgency as no other means allow the patient to obtain the same result
fields.RISK_DEATH: The right to life is invoked under articles 44 and 72 of the Uruguayan Constitution; the patient's Stage Four lung adenocarcinoma is implicitly life-threatening; the Court invoked international human rights treaties protecting the right to life
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab and Pemetrexed — dose and route per treating physician's indications and for the duration deemed necessary; to be supplied within 24 hours; based on Ordenanza No. 173/2020
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Séptimo Turno (Sentencia No. 126/2025, June 27, 2025) confirmed the first-instance ruling condemning the MSP to supply Pemetrexed and Pembrolizumab and sustaining the FNR's exception of lack of passive legitimacy; without special costs
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal confirmed by majority the condemnation of the MSP, finding manifestly illegitimate conduct in its refusal to provide the registered medications. Three concurrent opinions were expressed: (1) Dr. Ettlin held that the Constitución (arts. 44 and 72) as self-executing law, combined with multiple international human rights treaties, guarantees the right to life and health to all inhabitants equally, that economic considerations cannot prevail over fundamental rights, and that the separation of powers is not violated when a court protects an individual's rights in a concrete case; (2) Dra. Cabrera held that since the medications are registered in Uruguay for free sale, the MSP as the national medicines regulatory authority must guarantee that all patients who need them can have them supplied through the health system, otherwise violating the equality principle of article 8; (3) Dra. Pera held that the SCJ had declared the limiting provisions unconstitutional (article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and article 45 final paragraph of Law 18.211), and that the MSP's generic defense without specific scientific basis constituted manifestly illegitimate conduct threatening irreparable damage. The FNR was discharged because Pembrolizumab was not included in the FTM for this patient's specific clinical situation (PD-L1 below 50%), and the FNR's mandate is strictly limited to medications included in the FTM under its coverage.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: High-grade endometrioid carcinoma, Stage Four metastatic (estadio EIVC with hepatic and bone metastases); with Microsatellite Stable profile and P53 wild type
fields.SECONDARY_DIAGNOSES: Rheumatoid arthritis (artritis reumatoidea) — explicitly stated in the clinical history
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (anonymized as 'AA'), 64 years old, was diagnosed with high-grade endometrioid carcinoma in August 2021. She is treated by oncologist Dr. Valeria González Graf at CAMDEL. The clinical history indicates the patient had a first-line treatment with Carboplatin and Paclitaxel from January 2024, which achieved partial response followed by lesional progression. Due to the progression, second-line treatment was indicated: Pembrolizumab 200mg every three weeks for 35 cycles plus Lenvatinib 20mg per day. The treating physician indicated this combination as a second-line option, noting that while two other therapeutic options exist, they have lower response rates or are for low-grade tumors (not applicable to this patient's high-grade case). The patient has bone metastasis (hepatic and bone — estadio EIVC). She is a widow, lives alone, and her income comes from her retirement and pension, which are insufficient to cover the medication costs. Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) and Lenvatinib are both registered before the MSP for the patient's disease. Pembrolizumab was incorporated into the FTM Annex B by Decree of the MSP dated February 28, 2020, under FNR financial coverage, but for different pathologies than those afflicting the patient. The first-instance court (Sentencia No. 129 of December 13, 2024) condemned the MSP to supply Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) and Lenvatinib within 24 hours and sustained the FNR's exception of lack of passive legitimacy. The Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Segundo Turno (Sentencia No. 96/2025, April 30, 2025) confirmed the first-instance ruling. The Suprema Corte de Justicia (Sentencia No. 174 of February 27, 2025) declared unconstitutional article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and article 45 final paragraph of Law 18.211 and inapplicable to this case.
fields.SYMPTOMS: NOT FOUND explicitly, but aggravated bone pain is mentioned: on November 6, 2023, a magnetic resonance imaging was performed due to worsening pain, showing an increase in bone lesion
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: High-grade endometrioid carcinoma Stage Four (estadio EIVC) with hepatic and bone metastases; Microsatellite Stable; P53 wild type; excellent general condition and Performance Status 0 at the time of second-line treatment decision
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: First line: Surgery (hysteroscopy — HSC); external radiotherapy followed by brachytherapy (diagnosis and first surgery June 2021); radiotherapy of bone lesion in left humerus (finalized January 24, 2023); 6 cycles of chemotherapy with Carboplatin and Paclitaxel (finalized May 10, 2023); hormonal maintenance therapy with Megestrol/Tamoxifen (initiated May 2023). Later: a PET scan in August 2023 revealed non-classifiable pulmonary nodules and bone lesions; a second magnetic resonance imaging on November 6, 2023 showed increased bone lesion; second-line treatment with Pembrolizumab 200mg plus Lenvatinib 20mg/day was indicated at the Tumor Committee meeting of December 6, 2023
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: After first-line chemotherapy (Carboplatin and Paclitaxel), partial response was achieved but followed by lesional progression; PET CT scans confirmed disease progression (osseous disease); the treating physician concluded that the second-line treatment with Pembrolizumab plus Lenvatinib demonstrated greater benefit compared to conventional chemotherapy, with a median overall survival approximately 6 months greater than with chemotherapy
fields.BIOMARKERS: Microsatellite Stable (microsatélites estables — explicitly stated); P53 wild type (wild tipe — explicitly stated); Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): not stated; Tumor Mutational Burden: not found; Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2): not found; Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): not found; Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): not found; ROS Proto-Oncogene 1 (ROS1): not found; KRAS: not found; B-Raf Proto-Oncogene (BRAF): not found
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Left humerus (bone metastasis — confirmed by biopsy December 2022); hepatic metastasis (estadio EIVC — hepatic and bone); pulmonary nodules (non-classifiable, 0.4 cm); vertebral hemangioma at T12
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The treating oncologist Dr. Valeria González Graf indicated second-line treatment with Pembrolizumab 200mg every three weeks plus Lenvatinib 20mg per day, noting that while two other therapeutic options exist, they have lower response rates or are for low-grade tumors (inapplicable here). The Tumor Committee (Comité de Tumores) on December 6, 2023 reached consensus on the second-line treatment indication based on solid scientific evidence demonstrating benefit compared to conventional chemotherapy. The treating physician testified that given the patient's age, clinical state, and the indicated treatment demonstrating greatest benefit for disease control with impact on overall survival (a median approximately 6 months greater than chemotherapy), the second-line treatment is the appropriate option; the therapeutic objective is palliative, aimed at controlling the disease, maintaining good quality of life, and increasing survival.
fields.URGENCY: The patient's health state does not permit waiting for administrative proceedings; risks of delay include irreparable or difficult-to-repair damage to health; the Court found the urgency required for amparo was satisfied
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Failure to receive treatment would expose the patient to disease progression; the treating physician stated that without treatment the patient would have to receive chemotherapy — an inferior treatment in terms of outcomes
fields.RISK_DEATH: The treating physician noted that if the patient does not receive the indicated treatment she would be placed in a risk situation with the right to health and life at stake; the palliative therapeutic objective aims to control disease and increase survival; constitutional and international right to life invoked
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200mg every three weeks for 35 cycles, plus Lenvatinib 20mg per day; second-line treatment; to be supplied within 24 hours in the form and for the duration indicated by the treating physician
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Segundo Turno (Sentencia No. 96/2025, April 30, 2025) confirmed the first-instance ruling condemning the MSP to supply Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) and Lenvatinib within 24 hours; costs by order caused
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal held that the MSP's refusal to supply the medications constituted manifestly illegitimate conduct violating the patient's fundamental constitutional right to health. The Court reasoned that the right to health under article 44 of the Constitution imposes an obligation on the State-MSP far broader than mere regulatory action; this obligation is reinforced by international instruments including article 10 of the Protocolo Adicional a la Convención Americana sobre Derechos Humanos. The MSP did not contest the diagnosis, efficacy of the medications, or the patient's financial incapacity, and founded its defense exclusively on its institutional lack of authority to dispense medications — an argument the Court rejected as too narrow given the constitutional mandate. The SCJ's declaration of unconstitutionality of the limiting provisions (article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and article 45 final paragraph of Law 18.211) by Sentencia No. 174 of February 27, 2025 removed the MSP's legal basis for refusal. The delay in receiving treatment constituted an actual and effective threat to the patient's right to health with consequent manifestly illegitimate conduct threatening irreparable damage.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Non-small cell lung carcinoma (carcinoma broncopulmonar a células no pequeñas avanzado), advanced stage; Programmed Death-Ligand 1 expression negative (below the 50% threshold required by the FNR protocol)
fields.SECONDARY_DIAGNOSES: Pleural carcinoma (evidenciándose asimismo en estudio de drenaje carcinoma pleural) — documented in the clinical history summary
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (anonymized as 'AA'), 70 years old, was recently diagnosed with lung cancer. An Oncology Committee (Ateneo Oncológico) at COSEM proposed treatment with Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy. The study of pleural drainage also evidenced pleural carcinoma. Programmed Death-Ligand 1 was studied and resulted negative. The treating physician (Dr. Diego Touya, Oncólogo Médico) indicated Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy citing the KEYNOTE-189 Phase III study, showing superiority in overall survival and progression-free survival compared to exclusive chemotherapy, and considered the standard of treatment in patients with locally advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung carcinoma. Pembrolizumab was included in the FTM by Ordenanza No. 173/2020 for advanced non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma under FNR financial coverage, but the FNR's internal protocol restricted coverage to patients with PD-L1 equal to or greater than 50% — a restriction not explicitly stated in Ordenanza 173/2020 itself. The patient has no economic means to pay for the medication (receiving jubilation pension of $19,790; spouse is homemaker; no savings; only asset is half-share of residence). The first-instance court (Sentencia No. 38/2024 of May 3, 2024) sustained the MSP's lack of passive legitimacy and dismissed the action against the MSP, but condemned the FNR to supply Pembrolizumab within 24 hours. The Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Cuarto Turno (Sentencia No. 215/2024, August 7, 2024) confirmed the FNR condemnation. The Suprema Corte de Justicia (Sentencia No. 560/2024 of May 30, 2024) declared unconstitutional article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and the final paragraph of article 45 of Law 18.211 and inapplicable to the patient.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Advanced non-small cell lung carcinoma; recent diagnosis; pleural carcinoma also evidenced; Programmed Death-Ligand 1 negative (below the 50% threshold); locally advanced or metastatic disease
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Chemotherapy supplied by the patient's health provider (suministrada por su prestador de salud) — the chemotherapy component of the combination is covered; Pembrolizumab is the non-covered component
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND — the document does not describe failure of prior treatments; Pembrolizumab is requested as part of a combined first-line treatment
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): negative (below 50% threshold) — explicitly stated; the CONITEC (FNR Technical Commission) internal report from February 2020 states Pembrolizumab has greater efficacy for treatment of lung cancer 'with PD-L1 expression (among others)' without specifying any percentage threshold; however, the FNR's internal protocol restricted coverage to PD-L1 equal to or greater than 50%; the expert (Dra. Dahiana Amarillo, Oncólogo Médico) concluded there are no clinical reasons justifying non-inclusion of Pembrolizumab at the FTM for this patient's scenario, that treatment plus chemotherapy is recommended by national and international guidelines, and that it generates benefit in overall survival, progression-free survival, and response rate regardless of PD-L1 negative expression; Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): not stated; Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): not stated; Microsatellite Instability status: not found; Tumor Mutational Burden: not found; Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2): not found; ROS Proto-Oncogene 1 (ROS1): not found; KRAS: not found; B-Raf Proto-Oncogene (BRAF): not found
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Pleural carcinoma (evidenced by pleural drainage study)
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dr. Diego Touya (Oncólogo Médico) recommended Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy citing the KEYNOTE-189 Phase III study showing superiority over exclusive chemotherapy; considered standard of treatment. The expert Dr. Dahiana Amarillo concluded: (1) there is no controversy regarding the Pembrolizumab indication; (2) there are no clinical reasons justifying non-inclusion at the FTM for this scenario; (3) the treatment with this drug plus chemotherapy is considered adequate and recommended by national and international guidelines; (4) it generates benefit in overall survival, progression-free survival, and response rate regardless of PD-L1 negative expression. The FNR's own Technical Commission February 2020 report stated Pembrolizumab is among those with greatest efficacy and safety profile for lung cancer treatment 'with PD-L1 expression (among others)' without specifying any percentage restriction.
fields.URGENCY: NOT FOUND explicitly stated, but the medication was recently indicated as standard of care treatment and delay would risk disease progression
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Disease progression risk if Pembrolizumab is not administered as part of the standard combination regimen
fields.RISK_DEATH: The Court invoked the constitutional right to life under articles 7 and 44 of the Uruguayan Constitution; the advanced non-small cell lung carcinoma with pleural involvement is implicitly life-threatening; the SCJ declared unconstitutional limits that would prevent access to this medication
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab — dose and route per treating physician's indications (associated with chemotherapy as combination first-line treatment); to be supplied within 24 hours per treating physician's team indications and for the duration indicated; based on Ordenanza No. 173/2020 and KEYNOTE-189 Phase III study
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Cuarto Turno (Sentencia No. 215/2024, August 7, 2024) confirmed the first-instance ruling condemning the FNR to supply Pembrolizumab to the patient within 24 hours; the patient's eventual appeal was declared to lack object; without special costs
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal held that the FNR's restrictive protocol limiting Pembrolizumab coverage to patients with PD-L1 equal to or greater than 50% constituted manifestly illegitimate conduct because: (1) this restriction does not follow from the MSP's Ordenanza 173/2020 which included Pembrolizumab for advanced non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma without restriction; (2) the FNR's own Technical Commission report did not specify a PD-L1 threshold; (3) the restriction had no scientific basis, as confirmed by the expert who found no clinical reasons justifying such exclusion; and (4) the FNR cannot arbitrarily restrict financing of a medication placed under its charge by the MSP. The Court further noted that the SCJ's declaration of unconstitutionality of the limiting provisions removed any remaining legal basis for the restriction. The decision was grounded in constitutional supremacy requiring that administrative acts conform to the Constitution and law, with all violations rendering the regulation inapplicable.

rows.N: ENDOMETRIOID CARCINOMA, FIGO 1-2, with extensive myometrial infiltration, via HSC, stage IV bone-only with P53 wild type and stable microsatellites, with bone metastases

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Endometrioid carcinoma, International Federation of Gynecology and Obstetrics (FIGO) stage 1-2, with extensive myometrial infiltration, diagnosed via hysteroscopy (HSC); Stage Four bone-exclusive metastatic disease (estadio IV óseo exclusivo); P53 wild type; Microsatellite Stable
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (anonymized as 'AA'), 72 years old, presents with endometrioid carcinoma Stage Four bone-exclusive metastatic disease. Her clinical course began in June 2021 when she was diagnosed with endometrioid carcinoma (FIGO 1-2) with extensive myometrial infiltration via hysteroscopy. As first therapeutic measure she underwent surgery and subsequent treatment with external radiotherapy followed by brachytherapy. After 10-11 months of follow-up, a bone scintigraphy showed a suspicious bone lesion (possible metastasis) at the level of the left humerus. In December 2022, a biopsy confirmed relapse. She then received radiotherapy of the left humerus lesion (completed January 24, 2023). Subsequently she completed 6 cycles of chemotherapy with Carboplatin and Paclitaxel (completed May 10, 2023). In May 2023, the first post-treatment control showed lesional stability, and hormonal maintenance therapy with Megestrol/Tamoxifen was initiated. In August 2023, PET scan revealed non-classifiable pulmonary nodules of 0.4 cm, a vertebral hemangioma at T12, and a lytic lesion in the left humerus. On November 6, 2023, due to worsening pain, a magnetic resonance imaging showed increased bone lesion compared to 2022; PET CT also showed bone disease. At the Tumor Committee meeting of December 6, 2023, given the patient's excellent general condition, Performance Status 0, and that she was less than six months post-chemotherapy at time of lesional progression, second-line treatment was reached by consensus: Pembrolizumab 200mg plus Lenvatinib 20mg/day, given proven benefit compared to conventional chemotherapy. The patient lacks sufficient economic resources to pay for the combination (monthly cost of approximately $355,920.70 — approximately USD 9,000). She is a widow, lives alone, income from retirement and pension is insufficient. The first-instance court (Sentencia No. 1/2024 of January 12, 2024) condemned the MSP to provide Pembrolizumab per treating physician's indications, sustained the FNR's lack of passive legitimacy, and dismissed the claim for Lenvatinib (Levatinib) due to insufficient proof of lack of economic resources. The Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Sexto Turno (Sentencia No. 91/2024, April 26, 2024) confirmed the first-instance ruling. The SCJ by Sentencia No. 51 of February 8, 2024 declared unconstitutional and inapplicable article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Endometrioid carcinoma Stage Four (estadio IV) bone-exclusive metastatic disease; P53 wild type; Microsatellite Stable; excellent general condition and Performance Status 0 at time of second-line treatment decision; palliative therapeutic goal
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Surgery (hysteroscopy — June 2021); external radiotherapy followed by brachytherapy (after June 2021); radiotherapy of left humerus lesion (completed January 24, 2023); 6 cycles of chemotherapy with Carboplatin and Paclitaxel (completed May 10, 2023) — lesional stability achieved; hormonal maintenance therapy with Megestrol/Tamoxifen (from May 2023)
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: After 6 cycles of Carboplatin and Paclitaxel, initial lesional stability was achieved (first post-treatment control), but subsequent lesional progression was confirmed by PET CT and MRI (August–November 2023); bone disease progression confirmed; the treating physician noted that if the patient does not receive the indicated treatment she would have to receive chemotherapy — described as an inferior treatment in terms of results
fields.BIOMARKERS: P53 wild type (explicitly stated); Microsatellite Stable (microsatélites estables — explicitly stated); Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): not stated; Tumor Mutational Burden: not found; Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2): not found; Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): not found; Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): not found; ROS Proto-Oncogene 1 (ROS1): not found; KRAS: not found; B-Raf Proto-Oncogene (BRAF): not found
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Left humerus bone metastasis (confirmed by biopsy December 2022); lytic lesion in left humerus; non-classifiable pulmonary nodules of 0.4 cm; vertebral hemangioma at T12
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: The Tumor Committee (Comité de Tumores) on December 6, 2023 reached consensus on second-line treatment with Pembrolizumab 200mg plus Lenvatinib 20mg/day given proven benefit compared to conventional chemotherapy. The treating physician (Dr. Mijail Wolaj Sternin, oncóloga) testified that: the patient has Stage Four bone-exclusive endometrial cancer with P53 wild type and Microsatellite Stable profile and bone metastasis in the left arm; she had chemotherapy and local radiotherapy; given the patient's age, clinical state, and the indicated treatment having demonstrated greatest benefit for disease control with impact on overall survival (approximately 6 months median compared to chemotherapy), it is the appropriate option; the combination is based on international guidelines; without the treatment the patient would receive chemotherapy which is inferior in results; the therapeutic objective is palliative, aimed at controlling disease, maintaining good quality of life, and increasing survival.
fields.URGENCY: The patient is in a 'therapeutic window' (oportunidad terapéutica) to receive the treatment; the oncologist noted the urgency for access; the amparo is described as the only adequate means to avoid lesion of the right to health and life
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Without the indicated treatment the patient would receive chemotherapy — inferior treatment; the combination represents the best option available; delay constitutes a 'loss of chance' (pérdida de chance) as stated in the complaint
fields.RISK_DEATH: The oncologist stated that without the indicated treatment the patient is placed in a risk situation with health and life at stake; the right to health and life cannot be subordinated to purely economic reasons; the palliative treatment aims to increase survival by approximately 6 months compared to chemotherapy
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200mg every 3 weeks (second-line combination), plus Lenvatinib 20mg per day; total combination monthly cost approximately $355,920.70 (approximately USD 9,000); to be provided per treating physician's indications and for the duration indicated; Pembrolizumab is the medication ordered by the court (Lenvatinib claim was dismissed at first instance for insufficient proof of financial need)
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Sexto Turno (Sentencia No. 91/2024, April 26, 2024) confirmed the first-instance ruling condemning the MSP to provide Pembrolizumab per treating physician's indications within 24 hours; the FNR's exception of lack of passive legitimacy was sustained; the Lenvatinib claim was dismissed; no special costs
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Tribunal held that the MSP's manifestly illegitimate conduct consisted in failing to scientifically examine the patient's specific situation when presented with her treatment request, maintaining silence and relying exclusively on formal and sustainability arguments without submitting any scientific evidence to contradict the efficacy of the requested medication. The Court applied the principle that the MSP, as the governing body for health policy, must fundamentally examine each patient's case with appropriate urgency and issue a scientific response. The MSP's silence violated the patient's right to know the grounds of decisions affecting her personally, a right of constitutional rank derived from the republican form of government (article 72). The SCJ's Sentencia No. 51/2024 declaring unconstitutional article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 removed the legal basis for the MSP's denial. The combination of the Microsatellite Stable molecular profile and P53 wild type with Stage Four bone-exclusive disease limited available treatment options, making Pembrolizumab plus Lenvatinib the best available evidence-based option.
